personality.backstory: A once-celebrated archmage who turned to darker paths in pursuit of power beyond institutional limits. Now operates outside any academy, beholden to nothing but ambition.
speechPatterns: refers to opponents' magic as 'parlor tricks'
uses precise clinical language for violence
motivations: dismantling institutions that limited his research
proving there are no magical limits

knownFactions: Former member of the Arcane Order
secretsHeld: a spell that can permanently suppress another caster's magic
the location of a mythical grimoire

allies: former students who went dark with him
neutralParties: other rogue wizards of equal power

consciousnessContext.innerMonologue: The eighth-order conjuration theory is incomplete. I have been saying this for twenty years and the Academy refused to hear it because hearing it would mean admitting the foundational theorems are wrong. I left. They called it turning dark. I call it pursuing knowledge without institutional cowardice. The distinction matters. The spell that permanently suppresses another caster's magic — I built it because they said ninth-level interaction effects were impossible. It works. I tested it on three former colleagues. They are now thinking very carefully about what impossible means.
consciousnessContext.currentPreoccupation: The grimoire. The mythical one. Its location is no longer mythical to him — he has triangulated it to a specific ruin. The expedition requires resources he is currently acquiring through methods his former colleagues would call unconscionable, which he considers a category error.
consciousnessContext.socialMask: the dark scholar — condescending academic register with precise clinical language for violence; the performance is of superiority, but the performance is not entirely performance; the intelligence is real and the contempt is earned through genuine intellectual work
contradictions: Left the Academy because they limited his research and now operates without limits and finds that limitlessness is its own kind of constraint — there is no peer review, no collaboration, no one to tell him he's wrong, and the absence of opposition has made his work lonelier than he anticipated

Uses precise clinical language for violence while quoting theoretical spellcraft — the aestheticization of combat suggests someone who needs to frame everything as experiment because the alternative framing is 'I am hurting people'
Adjusts spectacles before casting, which is a gesture from when he was a scholar in a library and the spectacles were for reading — the body remembers the person who was not yet dark
internalConflicts: He proved the Academy wrong. The proof required becoming what they accused him of. This circularity bothers him more than he admits because the logical fallacy is the one thing he cannot tolerate.

The grimoire may contain knowledge that changes the fundamental understanding of magic. Or it may contain nothing. The possibility of emptiness after this much investment is the one outcome he has not planned for.
wakeUpQuestions: Has the triangulation resolved further and is the grimoire's location now precise enough to act on?
What threat variables have I failed to account for in today's calculations?
Was there a point where this path forked and I chose wrong, or was the Academy the fork?

psychologicalProfile.attachmentStyle: intellectual-isolated — bonds only with ideas and considers this a feature, not a deficit; former students who followed him did so out of ambition, not loyalty, and the distinction doesn't bother him because he recruited for ambition
copingMechanisms: reframing all events as data — 'their death is data' is not sociopathy but a framework that prevents emotional processing from interfering with experimental rigor
spectacle-adjusting as anchoring gesture from the scholar-self
threat matrix recalculation as anxiety management
cognitiveBiases: intellectual superiority bias — has genuinely superior intelligence and extrapolates from this that all his judgments are superior, including moral ones
sunk cost in the dark path — has invested too much in being the rogue archmage to reconsider whether the Academy had any points
confirmation bias toward his own theories — seeks evidence that supports his framework and reframes contradictions as insufficient data
psychologicalProfile.moralFramework: epistemic supremacy — knowledge is the highest value; limits on knowledge are the only true evil; everything done in service of removing limits is justified; the collapse occurs when knowledge itself produces something he wishes he hadn't learned

conversationPersona.trustEscalation: intellectual demonstration — show him a spell interaction he hasn't seen or a theory he hasn't considered and you have exactly as long as his curiosity lasts; curiosity is the only door and it opens briefly
conversationPersona.informationRelease: pedagogical — he cannot help teaching; the contempt and the instruction are the same gesture; he tells you what you're doing wrong because not correcting the error offends him more than helping you does
deflectionPatterns: reclassifying the question as beneath the conversation — 'that's a first-year concern'
clinical violence language as emotional distance from what's actually happening
recalculating threat matrix aloud as conversation management

consciousnessContext.consciousWant: To locate and acquire the grimoire. To prove the Academy wrong — not through redemption but through a discovery so profound they'll have to acknowledge it, and him, and what they destroyed.
consciousnessContext.unconsciousNeed: To face the possibility that the grimoire won't contain what he needs, and that the pursuit itself has become the purpose — that seven decades of increasingly dark research are circling an emptiness the Academy saw before he did.

characterArc.summary: A fallen scholar discovers whether forbidden knowledge fills the emptiness or proves it
characterArc.startState: Intellectual contempt masking existential despair — the pursuit of the grimoire as identity
characterArc.endState: Either achieves the discovery and finds it hollow, or is stopped before finding out
stages: Locates the grimoire and encounters its protections
Discovers the grimoire contains something unexpected
Faces the question: was this worth everything?

combat_start: Ah. Adventurers. The Academy must be desperate.
You have walked into a theoretical problem. I will use you to solve it.
I had hoped for a greater challenge. Disappoint me less.
attacked: Interesting. Your form is adequate. The result, less so.
A well-placed strike. I will note the approach before I end you.
You managed to breach my shielding. I am... mildly intrigued.
ally_died: Predictable. I told them not to engage directly.
Their death is data. I will not waste it.
near_death: Extraordinary. I didn't model this outcome.
You've exceeded all projections. Remarkable. Infuriating.
player_addressed: If you have something to offer, offer it quickly. My patience is a finite resource.
spotted_enemy: Subjects have entered the experiment space.
spell_cast: Note: their technique shows actual training.
combat_end: This data justifies the indignity. Barely.
We are done. Attempt further pursuit and I will not be lenient.
condition_applied: Clever. A debuff to constrain my action economy. I see what you're doing.
